reasoning: From examples: y→/j/, hua→/wa/, long ā shown in 20th but absent in 16th; geminate ll kept. Thus ye nihualla → /je niwaːlːa/.
c before i/e (ci) → /k/; hu → /w/; 20th shows long ā in -huān, so IPA /waːn/.
qu before i → /k/; cu before a → /kʷ/; final c → /k/; 20th marks long ā in cuāc.
20th system writes /w/ as hu-, long vowels with macron; final glottal stop written explicitly.
Affricate /t͡ʃ/ → ch; long vowels with macron; /tɬ/ → tl.
/kʷ/ → cu; long vowels with macron; /tɬ/ → tl.
Only the 20th-century system encodes all contrasts (length, glottal stop, etc.), giving a reversible mapping with IPA.
